roles.description: Reads audit, export, admission, work-item, governance, and status evidence without user-management write power.
Reads repository, code, documentation, service context, and Ask/search evidence for granted workspaces, repositories, and source scopes.
Reads runtime status, freshness, service context, collector posture, and recovery diagnostics for granted scopes.
Bootstrap-capable owner with admin plus sensitive-data grants; owners can delegate or remove those grants after setup.
Reads sensitive cloud/IaC, secrets/IAM, supply-chain, audit, and security evidence for granted workspaces.
Delegated role for sensitive data classes without broad tenant administration.
Manages users, memberships, provider settings, roles, grants, tokens, and workspace settings without receiving sensitive data classes by default.

data_classes.description: Tenant, workspace, provider, bootstrap, and recovery administration metadata.
Ask/search evidence retrieval, citations, reasoning traces, and answer narration metadata.
Detailed audit decisions, denied-decision evidence, exports, and incident report material.
Static capability, surface, schema, and product contract metadata.
Cloud inventory, IaC resources, drift, import, and platform topology evidence.
Documentation facts, semantic observations, code hints, source policy, and derived semantic evidence.
Repository identifiers, language inventory, freshness, and coverage metadata.
Secrets, IAM, trust-chain, privilege posture, secret-access, and posture-gap evidence.
Services, workloads, deployment stories, incidents, runtime topology, and observability coverage.
Source code, file content, entity content, code graph evidence, and citation snippets.
Runtime health, readiness, collector, ingester, governance, and freshness status.
SBOM, image, package, vulnerability, scanner, reachability, security alert, and CI/CD correlation evidence.
Personal token, service-principal token, expiry, rotation, owner, and last-used metadata.

permission_families.description: Recovery, extension, component, backfill, replay, dead-letter, bootstrap, and break-glass administration.
Ask Eshu, semantic search, cited evidence retrieval, reasoning trace, and answer narration posture.
Audit, export, admission decision, work-item evidence, denied-decision inspection, and incident/audit report reads.
Cloud inventory, IaC quality, drift, import, unmanaged resource, replatforming, and cloud-resource impact reads.
Documentation facts/findings, semantic evidence, semantic extraction status, and semantic observation reads.
User, invitation, membership, provider, MFA, tenant, and workspace administration. Runtime surfaces land in the identity implementation slices.
Runtime status, freshness, governance status, collector extraction readiness, surface inventory, schema inventory, and query workflow catalogs.
Bounded dead-letter and input-invalid-quarantine operator triage reads over fact work items and reducer_input_invalid_facts, scoped to granted repositories or source scopes.
Repository, source, content, code graph, code-quality, relationships, evidence citation, dependency, codeowners ownership, and visualization reads.
Role, grant, capability-catalog, effective-permission, and data-class administration.
Secrets/IAM trust-chain, privilege posture, secret access path, posture gap, and posture summary reads.
Service, workload, runtime topology, deployment story, operational context, incidents, Kubernetes correlations, service catalog, and observability coverage.
Supply-chain, SBOM, image, package, dependency, reachability, security-alert, and CI/CD correlation reads.
Personal-token and service-principal token lifecycle. Runtime token surfaces land in the token implementation slice.

custom_policy.note: Custom policy language is intentionally deferred for v1; the catalog keeps actions, data classes, roles, and scope levels explicit so a future policy language can reference the same closed vocabulary.

entries.maturity_reason: Ask is default-off and requires ESHU_ASK_ENABLED plus an agent_reasoning provider profile; without both, the HTTP and MCP surfaces return unavailable instead of running the engine.
known_gaps: Scoped-token support, SSE streaming, and Tier-2 sandbox wiring are planned follow-ups.

entries.maturity_reason: CI/CD run correlations are populated by the ci_cd_run collector polling the provider run API (for example GitHub Actions), which is off in a default deploy (no compose service, Helm enabled: false). It is enabled only by supplying ESHU_COLLECTOR_INSTANCES_JSON with a ci_cd_run instance plus a provider token; a default git-only deploy returns an empty page.

entries.display_name: Code To Cloud Trace Exposure Path
Iac Management Propose Terraform Import Plan
Operator Reducer Input Invalid Facts List

entries.maturity_reason: Package-registry correlations join git manifest evidence to registry package identities, so a correlation row persists only when the package_registry collector has populated those identities. That collector is off in a default deploy (no compose service, Helm enabled: false) and enabled only by supplying ESHU_COLLECTOR_INSTANCES_JSON with a package_registry instance plus registry credentials; a default git-only deploy returns an empty page.

Package-native registry dependencies are populated by the package_registry collector calling live registry APIs, which is off in a default deploy (no compose service, Helm enabled: false). It is enabled only by supplying ESHU_COLLECTOR_INSTANCES_JSON with a package_registry instance plus registry credentials; a default git-only deploy returns an empty page.

Package-registry package identities are populated by the package_registry collector calling live registry APIs, which is off in a default deploy (no compose service, Helm enabled: false). It is enabled only by supplying ESHU_COLLECTOR_INSTANCES_JSON with a package_registry instance plus registry credentials; a default git-only deploy returns an empty page.

Package-registry versions are populated by the package_registry collector calling live registry APIs, which is off in a default deploy (no compose service, Helm enabled: false). It is enabled only by supplying ESHU_COLLECTOR_INSTANCES_JSON with a package_registry instance plus registry credentials; a default git-only deploy returns an empty page.

entries.display_name: Platform Impact Container Image Tag History

entries.maturity_reason: C# value-flow reachability is gated by ESHU_EMIT_DATAFLOW, which is off by default; the matrix cannot express an env-var gate, so the profiles are unsupported until an operator opts in.
known_gaps: Disabled unless ESHU_EMIT_DATAFLOW is set; when enabled, value-flow taint evidence is derived (partial), never exact, and does not change impact truth.

ADO.NET sink receiver-type inference is intraprocedural and explicit-type only: a sink whose receiver is a var/implicit-typed local with no declared type is conservatively not matched (honesty over false positive).
Reachability is prioritization evidence on the impact envelope; not_called is emitted only with ecosystem-specific scanner semantics, otherwise unknown.

entries.maturity_reason: Java value-flow reachability is gated by ESHU_EMIT_DATAFLOW, which is off by default; the matrix cannot express an env-var gate, so the profiles are unsupported until an operator opts in.
known_gaps: Disabled unless ESHU_EMIT_DATAFLOW is set; when enabled, value-flow taint evidence is derived (partial), never exact, and does not change impact truth.

entries.maturity_reason: JavaScript value-flow reachability is gated by ESHU_EMIT_DATAFLOW, which is off by default; the matrix cannot express an env-var gate, so the profiles are unsupported until an operator opts in.
known_gaps: Disabled unless ESHU_EMIT_DATAFLOW is set; when enabled, value-flow taint evidence is derived (partial), never exact, and does not change impact truth.

Bounded/partial: the JVM reducer family derives Maven/Gradle reachability from stored parser/SCIP package-API evidence (jvm_package_api_reachable), not a full call graph, so it never proves not_called and stays a local graph-backed preview.

entries.maturity_reason: Python value-flow reachability is gated by ESHU_EMIT_DATAFLOW, which is off by default; the matrix cannot express an env-var gate, so the profiles are unsupported until an operator opts in.
known_gaps: Disabled unless ESHU_EMIT_DATAFLOW is set; when enabled, value-flow taint evidence is derived (partial), never exact, and does not change impact truth.

known_gaps: Disabled unless ESHU_EMIT_DATAFLOW is set; when enabled, value-flow taint evidence is derived (partial), never exact, and does not change impact truth.

Supply Chain Container Image Identities Aggregate
Supply Chain Container Image Identities List
entries.maturity_reason: Container image identities persist only when a git or cloud image ref matches an oci_registry digest or tag observation, so they require the oci_registry collector, which is off in a default deploy (no compose service, Helm enabled: false). It is enabled only by supplying ESHU_COLLECTOR_INSTANCES_JSON with an oci_registry instance plus container-registry credentials; a default git-only deploy returns an empty page.

Supply-chain impact findings are seeded by the vulnerability_intelligence and security_alert collectors, which are off in a default deploy (no compose service, Helm enabled: false). They are enabled only by supplying ESHU_COLLECTOR_INSTANCES_JSON with those instances plus advisory-feed or provider credentials; a default git-only deploy returns an empty page whose readiness envelope reports not_configured rather than a false fresh-zero.

entries.display_name: Supply Chain Sbom Attestation Attachments Aggregate
Supply Chain Sbom Attestation Attachments List
entries.maturity_reason: SBOM and attestation attachments are populated by the sbom_attestation collector, which is off in a default deploy (no compose service, Helm enabled: false). It is enabled only by supplying ESHU_COLLECTOR_INSTANCES_JSON with the sbom_attestation instance plus SBOM document URLs or OCI referrer credentials; a default git-only deploy returns an empty page.

entries.display_name: Supply Chain Security Alert Reconciliations Aggregate
Supply Chain Security Alert Reconciliations List
entries.maturity_reason: Provider security alert reconciliations are built from security_alert.repository_alert facts emitted only by the security_alert collector, which is off in a default deploy (no compose service, Helm enabled: false). It is enabled only by supplying ESHU_COLLECTOR_INSTANCES_JSON with a security_alert instance plus provider credentials; a default git-only deploy returns an empty page.
